eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
fen: noun. A type of wetland fed by ground water and runoff, containing peat below the waterline, characteristically alkaline.
fern: noun. Any of a group of some twenty thousand species of vascular plants classified in the division Pteridophyta that lack seeds and
fie: interjection. Sometimes followed by on or upon: used to express distaste, disgust, or outrage.
fin: noun. One of the appendages of a fish, used to propel itself and to manoeuvre / maneuver.
fine: adjective. Senses referring to subjective quality.
finer: adjective. comparative form of fine: more fine
fino: noun. The driest and palest type of traditional sherry.
fir: noun. A conifer of the genus Abies.
fire: noun. A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat
firer: noun. A person who fires a weapon; a shooter.
firn: noun. A type of old snow which has gone through multiple thaw and refreeze cycles and thus is made of numerous small icy grains, though it
fore: adjective. Former; occurring earlier (in some order); previous.
fro: adverb. From; away; back or backward.
froe: noun. A cleaving tool for splitting cask staves and shingles from a block of wood.
infer: verb. To introduce (something) as a reasoned conclusion; to conclude by reasoning or deduction, as from premises or evidence.
inferior: adjective. Lower in rank, status, or quality.
inro: noun. A small decorative box, hung from the sash of a kimono, for holding small objects.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
iron: noun. A common, inexpensive metal, silvery grey when untarnished, that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel: a
irone: noun. Any of several ketones, or a mixture of such, found in orris oil (oil extracted from iris roots), used as odorants in perfumes.
ironer: noun. Someone who irons, someone who does ironing.
neif: noun. A woman born in the state of serfdom; a female serf.
noir: adjective. Of or pertaining to film noir, or the atmosphere associated with that genre
nor: conjunction. And... not (introducing a negative statement, without necessarily following one).
nori: noun. A type of seaweed, a red alga, laver (genus Pyropia, including species P. yezoensis and P. tenera).
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
rein: noun. A strap or rope attached to a bridle or bit, used to control a horse, other animal or young child.
rif: verb. Alternative letter-case form of RIF.
rife: adjective. Widespread, common, prevalent, current (mainly of unpleasant or harmful things).
rifer: adjective. comparative form of rife: more rife
eir: determiner. Belonging to em, gender-neutral third-person singular possessive adjective, equivalent to the singular their and coordinate
feni: noun. An alcoholic drink made in Goa, India, from fermented cashew apples or coconut toddy (sap).
freon: noun. Any of several nonflammable refrigerants based on halogenated hydrocarbon including R-12, R-22, and R-23.
fri: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Western Frisian.
nef: noun. An extravagant table ornament and container used in the Middle Ages and Renaissance, made in the shape of a ship.
nief: noun. A serf or bondsman born into servitude.
nife: noun. The innermost layers or core of the Earth, composed chiefly of nickel and iron.
oie: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Okolie.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
orf: noun. An exanthemous disease caused by a parapox virus, occurring primarily in sheep and goats but also capable of infecting humans.
orfe: noun. A fish, the ide, Leuciscus idus.
rio: noun. Alternative form of ryō (“Japanese ounce”).
